more.e: I would like to have more cookies.

make.e: I can make a paper airplane.

may.d: possibility or permission to do something
may.e: You may have a cookie after dinner.

most.e: I like this toy the most out of all of them.

much.d: a large amount or degree of something
much.e: I have much homework to do tonight.

made.d: To create or put together something
made.e: The baker made a delicious cake for the party.

many.d: A large number of things or people
many.e: There were many different colors in the rainbow.

man.e: My dad is a very kind man.

must.d: To have to do something, it is required
must.e: You must be kind to others.

might.e: I might go to the park later.

money.d: What we use to buy things
money.e: I saved up my money to buy a new toy.

mr.e: My teacher's name is Mr. Smith.

mean.e: I didn't mean to make you sad.

making.e: I am making a birthday card for my mom.

music.d: Sounds that are pleasing to listen to
music.e: My favorite type of music is pop music.

men.e: The men in my family like to play sports.

means.d: a way of doing or getting something
means.e: The bus is the means to get to school.

mind.d: the part of you that thinks and has thoughts
mind.e: I need to clear my mind before the test.

makes.e: She makes a birthday card for her mom.

maybe.d: not sure if something will happen
maybe.e: Maybe we can go to the zoo this weekend.

members.d: People who belong to a group or club
members.e: The members of my soccer team all wear the same jersey.

matter.d: something that is important or needs attention
matter.e: It doesn't matter if you spill a little juice, we can clean it up.

million.e: I hope to have a million dollars someday.

months.d: One of the 12 parts of a year
months.e: My birthday is in three months.

major.e: The major event of the day was the school play.

minutes.e: There are 60 minutes in one hour.

morning.d: the early part of the day
morning.e: I eat breakfast in the morning before school.

move.e: The car moved slowly down the street.

myself.e: I can do this task by myself.

market.d: A place where people buy and sell things
market.e: I like to shop at the farmer's market.

meet.e: I will meet my friends at the park.

main.d: The most important or largest part of something
main.e: The main street in our town is very busy.

mother.d: A woman who has a child
mother.e: My mother reads me a bedtime story every night.

march.d: To walk with a steady rhythm, usually in a group
march.e: The soldiers marched together in the parade.

minister.d: A religious leader or government official
minister.e: The minister spoke at the community event.

moment.e: I need a moment to think about my answer.

miss.d: To fail to hit or reach something
miss.e: I tried to throw the ball but I missed the target.

member.d: Someone who belongs to a group or organization
member.e: I am a member of the school chess club.

media.d: Ways to share information, like TV and the internet
media.e: I saw the news about it on social media.

middle.d: The center or in between part of something
middle.e: The park is in the middle of the city.

month.d: One of the 12 parts of a year
month.e: My birthday is in the month of June.

meeting.d: When people come together for a discussion.
meeting.e: The class had a meeting to plan their school project.

met.d: To come together with someone or something.
met.e: I met my friend at the park to play.

model.d: A small copy of something that shows what the real thing looks like.
model.e: She built a model of a house out of Lego bricks.

medical.d: Having to do with doctors, hospitals, or health
medical.e: The doctor gave me some medical advice to help me feel better.

movie.d: A story shown on a screen, like in a theater
movie.e: My family likes to watch funny movies together on the weekends.

michael.d: A boy's name, like James or John
michael.e: My friend Michael likes to play soccer.

management.d: The way a person or group runs something
management.e: Good management is important for a business.

mine.e: This toy is mine, not my sister's.

match.d: Two things that are the same or go well together
match.e: My shoes and my shirt are a perfect match.

mom.d: The female parent in a family
mom.e: My mom made me a special birthday cake.

message.d: Words or information that are sent from one person to another.
message.e: I sent a message to my friend to meet me at the park.

modern.e: My family has a modern house with all the latest technology.

mark.d: A sign or symbol on something
mark.e: I can see a star mark on the map showing the park.

married.d: When two people become husband and wife
married.e: My aunt and uncle got married last summer.

missing.d: When something or someone is not where they should be
missing.e: The teacher noticed the missing book on the shelf.

movement.d: The act of moving from one place to another
movement.e: The steady movement of the clock's hands tells the time.

moved.d: To change the position of something
moved.e: The cat moved closer to the food.

material.d: Material is the stuff that things are made out of.
material.e: The chair is made of wooden material.

moving.d: Moving means to change location or position.
moving.e: The train was moving quickly down the tracks.

majority.d: Most of the group or amount
majority.e: The majority of the class passed the test.

miles.d: A long distance that you can measure
miles.e: It's about 10 miles from my house to the park.

minute.d: a short amount of time, 60 seconds
minute.e: I only need a minute to put on my shoes.

meant.d: When something is intended or supposed to be
meant.e: That toy was meant for my little brother.

marriage.d: When two people get married and decide to live together and take care of each other.
marriage.e: My parents have been in a happy marriage for many years.

master.d: Someone who is very good at doing something.
master.e: The artist is a master at painting beautiful landscapes.

machine.d: a device that uses power to do work
machine.e: The washing machine cleaned all my clothes.

manager.d: a person in charge of a business or group
manager.e: The store manager helped me find what I needed.

mission.d: an important job or goal that you must complete
mission.e: The scout troop's mission was to clean up the park.

male.e: My brother is a male and likes to play sports.

meaning.d: The important message or idea that something is trying to share.
meaning.e: The meaning of the story was to be kind to others.

mrs.d: A title for a married woman

mass.e: The storm brought a mass of dark clouds across the sky.

mentioned.d: When someone says or writes your name or a topic
mentioned.e: The teacher mentioned my name when I got the math question right.

mouth.d: The opening in your face where you can eat and talk
mouth.e: He opened his mouth to take a bite of his sandwich.

multiple.e: The student had multiple books to read for class.

memory.d: The ability to remember things from the past.
memory.e: My grandpa has a great memory and can tell stories from when he was young.

method.e: The teacher showed us a new method for solving math problems.

map.d: A drawing that shows where different places are.
map.e: We use a map to find our way when we go on a trip.

mental.d: Having to do with your thoughts and ideas.
mental.e: Solving puzzles is good for your mental skills.

mid.d: The middle part or time of something.
mid.e: We took a break in the mid-afternoon.

missed.d: When you fail to do, get, or see something.
missed.e: I missed my bus this morning and had to walk to school.

mobile.d: Something that can move or be moved around.
mobile.e: My baby cousin has a colorful mobile hanging over his crib.

mary.e: My friend Mary has the best smile.

monday.d: The first day of the week
monday.e: On Monday, I have soccer practice after school.

mostly.d: Mostly means something is true or happens a lot, but not all the time
mostly.e: I mostly like to play outside, but sometimes I read books too.

managed.d: To be in control of something or make it work.
managed.e: The teacher managed the classroom well.

matters.d: Things that are important or need attention.
matters.e: What matters most is that we all stay healthy and happy.

metal.d: A hard, shiny material used to make things like tools and jewelry.
metal.e: The necklace is made of shiny silver metal.

museum.d: A place where people can go to see interesting things like art or history.
museum.e: We visited the museum to learn about dinosaurs.

materials.d: The things used to make something.
materials.e: I need more art materials to finish my project.

measures.d: The size, amount, or degree of something.
measures.e: The teacher measures how tall the students are.

mention.d: To talk about or say something.
mention.e: The teacher didn't mention the homework assignment.

models.d: An example of something that shows how it works.
models.e: The toy car is a model of a real car.

mountain.e: We went hiking up a big mountain.

martin.e: My neighbor Martin has a big dog.

methods.e: The teacher showed us different methods to solve the math problem.

mexico.e: My family went on vacation to Mexico last summer.

mike.e: My uncle Mike is coming to my birthday party.

mad.e: The little boy was mad when his sister broke his toy.

magic.d: having special powers to make impossible things happen
magic.e: The magician did magic tricks that made the audience clap and cheer.

marketing.d: trying to sell something to people
marketing.e: The new toy company is doing a lot of marketing to get kids to want their toys.

measure.d: to find the size, amount, or degree of something
measure.e: The teacher helped the students measure the length of their desks.

ministry.d: a government department that deals with a specific area
ministry.e: The Ministry of Education makes rules for all the schools in the country.

moon.d: the round object in the sky that shines at night
moon.e: I like to look at the big, bright moon when I'm outside at night.

mail.d: Letters, packages, and other things sent through the post office
mail.e: I got a letter in the mail today.

medicine.d: Something you take to help you feel better when you are sick
medicine.e: The doctor gave me some new medicine.

movies.d: Shows or stories told with moving pictures and sound
movies.e: We're going to watch a funny movie tonight.

mainly.e: The main reason I like the park is because of the trees.

minimum.d: The smallest amount or number that is allowed or needed.
minimum.e: The store requires a minimum purchase of $5.

mix.d: To put different things together to make one thing.
mix.e: She likes to mix different colors of paint.

manner.d: the way something is done or how someone behaves
manner.e: The child had good manners at the dinner table.

markets.d: a place where people buy and sell things
markets.e: The farmer's market had lots of fresh fruits and vegetables.

milk.d: Milk is a white liquid that comes from cows and is good for you.
milk.e: I drink a glass of milk with my breakfast every day.

mistake.d: A mistake is when you do something wrong or not the way it should be done.
mistake.e: I made a mistake on my math homework, but I fixed it.

magazine.d: a book with news, stories, and pictures
magazine.e: I read my favorite magazine every month.

maintain.d: to keep something in good condition
maintain.e: We maintain the school's garden all year.

massive.e: The mountain is massive and tall.

mm.d: a sound used to show you are thinking or agreeing

mode.e: The school bus is the main mode of transportation for students.

ms.d: a title used for women, similar to 'Mr.' for men
ms.e: My teacher's name is Ms. Johnson.

ma.e: Ma gave me a big hug before I went to school.

max.d: the most or greatest amount that is allowed or possible
max.e: The teacher said the max number of students in the class is 25.

maximum.d: the largest or greatest amount possible
maximum.e: The restaurant has a maximum of 50 seats for customers.

meat.d: the part of an animal that people eat as food
meat.e: My mom cooked a delicious steak for dinner, which is a type of meat.

medium.d: something that is not too big or too small, but in the middle
medium.e: I ordered a medium-sized pizza because it was the right amount for my family.

minor.e: I had a minor cut on my finger, but it didn't need a bandage.

mixed.e: The salad had a mixed variety of vegetables in it.

marine.d: Having to do with the ocean
marine.e: The marine biologist studied the animals in the sea.

mayor.d: The leader of a city or town
mayor.e: The mayor helps make decisions for the whole city.

messages.d: Words or information sent to someone
messages.e: I sent a message to my mom to tell her I'm coming home

millions.e: There are millions of stars in the sky.

motion.d: The act of moving or changing position
motion.e: The car was in motion as it drove down the road.

moves.e: The dancer gracefully moves across the stage.

muslim.d: A person who follows the religion of Islam
muslim.e: My Muslim neighbor goes to the mosque to pray.

moments.e: I spent a few quiet moments reading my book.

manage.d: To take care of or control something.
manage.e: I need to manage my time better to finish my homework.

marked.d: When something is noticed or stands out.
marked.e: The handprint on the wall was clearly marked.

mountains.d: Very tall hills with steep sides.
mountains.e: The mountains are covered in snow at the top.

matches.d: small sticks used to start fires
matches.e: I keep the matches in the kitchen drawer.

mate.e: The two dogs were best mates.

matt.d: A type of finish that is not shiny.
matt.e: The new table had a nice matt finish.

ministers.d: People who lead a church or religious group.
ministers.e: The ministers led the special church service.

motor.d: A machine that makes something move.
motor.e: The car's motor helps the wheels turn.

multi.e: The multi-colored box had lots of different colors.

musical.d: Having to do with music or singing.
musical.e: The children played musical instruments in the school concert.

manufacturing.e: The toy factory does all the manufacturing for the company.

mess.d: a place or thing that is dirty or disorganized
mess.e: Cleaning up the messy art room was a big job.

mile.e: It's a mile to walk from the park to my house.

managers.d: People who lead and direct a business or team
managers.e: The managers at the store are very friendly.

manchester.e: My family visited Manchester last summer.

marks.d: Symbols or numbers that show how good someone did
marks.e: I got good marks on my spelling test.

mining.d: Digging in the ground to find valuable things
mining.e: Miners use special tools to do mining underground.

moral.d: Knowing the difference between right and wrong
moral.e: It's important to have good moral behavior.

meetings.d: When a group of people come together for a discussion.
meetings.e: The club had weekly meetings to plan events.

maintenance.d: the work needed to keep something in good condition
maintenance.e: Regular maintenance helps keep the car running well.

meal.d: the food you eat at a certain time of day
meal.e: We had a delicious meal for dinner.

moscow.e: Moscow is a very large and busy city.
